global_context.scene_description: A dramatic, slightly surreal advertising photograph of a hospital emergency room waiting room filled with sweating, miserable people in the middle of a heat emergency. At the very top of the image, large bold white text with a strong drop shadow reads 'THIS IS JULY WITHOUT A NEW AC.' in a heavy condensed sans-serif uppercase font. Below the headline, smaller white text reads 'Book now while we still have openings.' The waiting room has harsh fluorescent overhead lighting giving everything a slightly sickly green-blue cast. Rows of uncomfortable blue plastic waiting chairs are packed with people in summer clothes — tank tops, shorts — all visibly sweating, red-faced, using paper fans, magazines, and newspapers to fan themselves. Some are slumped over in misery. One person is holding a small battery-powered desk fan. The walls are a sterile institutional off-white with scuff marks. On the wall is a large LED digital display board showing wait information, reading 'HVAC EMERGENCY ROOM' as the header and below that 'CURRENT WAIT TIME: 3 WEEKS' in red LED text. A stressed nurse or receptionist is visible at a window in the background, overwhelmed. The floor is generic hospital vinyl tile. The overall mood is uncomfortable, claustrophobic, and miserable — this is what happens when you wait. At the very bottom of the image is a solid bright red horizontal banner spanning full width containing bold white uppercase text reading '$1,400 OFF. SUMMER BLOWOUT SALE.' and the ASH Cooling & Heating logo in white on the right side.

semantic_relationships: Headline at top frames the scene below: this is what July looks like
The display board is the key information point — 3 WEEKS wait time is the horror
The crowd of sweating people makes the scenario visceral and relatable
Harsh fluorescent lighting makes everyone look uncomfortable and sickly
The 'HVAC Emergency Room' sign reframes AC failure as a medical emergency — because in Arizona, it basically is
The nurse in the background being overwhelmed reinforces that help isn't coming fast
Red banner at bottom is the escape from this nightmare — act now and avoid this
